title: Adjustable Desktop Organizer
summary: Two identical A-frame shelf pieces from a single 1x6 board — sit them at any distance apart to span whatever you're organizing, or add a third for extra reach.
description:
This is one small, repeatable shape rather than a fixed design: a tall leg, a short leg, and a shelf between them, built twice from a single 1x6 board and then simply positioned wherever the gap between them is useful. There's no fixed spacing to get right — the whole point is that you set the two organizer pieces exactly as far apart as the thing you're organizing (books, folders, a laptop stand) needs them to be.

Each piece assembles the same way: mark the shelf location on both the tall and short leg, then nail or screw through with three fasteners per joint. Build two identical pieces before doing anything else, since matching them exactly is what makes the final organizer sit level.

For a longer shelf than a single 1x6 spans well, the shelf pieces can be cut up to 20" instead of 12" — and if an even longer run is needed, a third organizer piece slots in as a middle support, the same way a bookshelf gets a center standard on a long span.

materials.name: Brad nails, 1-1/4" and glue, or self-tapping star bit screws, 1-3/4"

cutList.note: Can be extended to 20" for a longer shelf. A 1x8 or 1x10 gives a deeper shelf.

steps.title: Cut the parts
steps.body: One 8 ft 1x6 makes both organizer pieces. Cut two tall legs 16", two short legs 11" and two shelves 12", all at the board's full 5 1/2" width — 78" of the 96", so there is room to re-cut a piece if a crosscut wanders. Cut the two of each part back to back at one saw setting so the pairs come out identical; the whole design depends on the two finished pieces matching. If you want a longer span, cut the shelves anywhere up to 20" instead of 12", and if you want a deeper shelf, cut the same three parts from a 1x8 or 1x10 instead.

steps.title: Mark and assemble the pieces
steps.body: Each piece is a shelf trapped between a tall leg and a short leg. Mark the shelf position on the inside face of both legs, measuring from the bottom end of each so the piece will stand level, and mark both pairs at the same time with their ends aligned so the second organizer cannot come out different from the first — the plan does not fix this height, so whatever you choose has to be repeated exactly. Stand the shelf between the legs and drive three fasteners through each leg's outside face into the shelf's end: 1-3/4" self-tapping screws, or 1-1/4" brads with glue. With a 12" shelf and 3/4" of leg on each side, each finished piece is 13 1/2" wide. Build both, set them side by side, and check the shelves land at the same height, or the organizer will rock.
materials: Brad nails, 1-1/4" and glue, or self-tapping star bit screws, 1-3/4"

steps.title: Position and finish
steps.body: Stand the two pieces on the desk with their tall legs facing the same way and set them as far apart as whatever they are holding needs — books, folders, a laptop. Nothing spans between them, so there is no fixed spacing to get right and nothing to re-cut if you change your mind later. For a run longer than the two pieces comfortably carry, build a third the same way and stand it in the middle as a support, exactly as a long bookshelf gets a center standard. Sand every face, break the edges, and stain or paint both pieces together so they finish the same.

images.alt: Simple adjustable wooden desktop organizer, two A-frame shelf pieces
